title: Pasta Networking - OUTBOUND Traffic does NOT work, INBOUND Traffic works
body:
### Issue Description

When using `pasta` Networking (at least using IPv6 Address Only) the following can be observed:
- OUTBOUND Traffic does NOT work
- INBOUND Traffic works (using Port Publishing / Mapping)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Patch Pasta According to my Comment in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22824 (otherwise it ABSOLUTELY will NOT work)
2. Start Podman Container (the minimal example Provided at https://passt.top/passt/about/#pasta_2 results in a timeout as well: `podman run --net=pasta --rm -ti alpine sh`)

Otherwise for my Debugging Container: https://github.com/luckylinux/docker-network-troubleshooting/

Which is first build then run using (tried several options, OUTBOUND traffic fails with each and every single one of them **except** using `slirp4netns` instead of `pasta`):
```
#!/bin/bash

# Determine toolpath if not set already
relativepath="./" # Define relative path to go from this script to the root level of the tool
if [[ ! -v toolpath ]]; then scriptpath=$(cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd ); toolpath=$(realpath --canonicalize-missing ${scriptpath}/${relativepath}); fi

# Load Configuration
libpath=$(readlink --canonicalize-missing "${toolpath}/includes")
source ${libpath}/functions.sh


# Optional argument
engine=${1-"podman"}

# Container Name
containername="docker-network-troubleshooting"

# Container Image
containerimage="docker-network-troubleshooting:debian-latest"

# Containers Configuration Folder to Map
containersconfigfolder="./containers"

# Options
opts=()

# Run in the Background
opts+=("-d")

# Podman 5.x with Pasta doesn't handle Networking Correctly
# Force to use slirp4netns
#opts+=("--network=slirp4netns")

# Debug Pasta
timestamp=$(date +"%Y%m%d_%Hh%M%S")
#opts+=("--network=pasta:--pcap,./tmp/${timestamp}.pcap")
#opts+=("--network=pasta:--ipv6-only,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2/80,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2/443,-t,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2/5001,-u,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2/5001")
#opts+=("--network=pasta:--ipv6-only,--outbound,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2")
#opts+=("--network=pasta:--ipv6-only,-a,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2")
opts+=("--network=pasta:--ipv6-only,-a,2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2,--pcap,./tmp/${timestamp}.pcap")

# Publish ports
opts+=("-p")
opts+=("[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2]:80:80/tcp")
opts+=("-p")
opts+=("[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2]:443:443/tcp")
opts+=("-p")
opts+=("[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX::2]:5201:5201/tcp")
opts+=("-p")
opts+=("[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:XXXX::2]:5201:5201/udp")

# Add Capacilities
opts+=("--cap-add")
opts+=("CAP_NET_RAW")

# Enable Infinite Loop
opts+=("-e")
opts+=("ENABLE_INFINITE_LOOP=true")

# Disable Automatic Test
opts+=("-e")
opts+=("ENABLE_AUTOMATIC_TEST=false")

# Load the Environment Variables into THIS Script
if [[ -f "./.env" ]]
then
   eval "$(shdotenv --env .env || echo \"exit $?\")"
fi

# Terminate and Remove Existing Containers if Any
${engine} stop --ignore ${containername}
${engine} rm --ignore ${containername}

# Run Image with Infinite Loop to prevent it from automatically terminating
#${engine} run --name=${containername} --env-file "./.env" localhost:5000/local/"${containerimage}"
echo "Executing: ${engine} run --name=${containername} ${opts[*]} localhost:5000/local/${containerimage}"
${engine} run --replace --name=${containername} ${opts[*]} localhost:5000/local/"${containerimage}"

# Open Interactive Shell with Container
#${engine} exec -it ${containername} /bin/bash

```

4. Try to perform a TCP Connection: `curl -6 ifconfig.me` for a Remote Server or `curl -6 https://[2XXX:XXXX:XXXX::0:1]` for my Local OPNSense Router Web Interface

Timeout will be reached.

### Describe the results you received

Timeout reached when running the above mentioned `curl` Commands.

Furthermore:
- `ping -6` (ICMPv6) of LAN Hosts from WITHIN the Container (even on a different Subnet) work correctly
- `ping -6` (ICMPv6) of WAN Hosts from WITHIN the Container Fails
- `traceroute6` work for LAN Hosts (TCP) BUT it takes around 30 seconds, while the reported TTL is shown as ~ 1.7 ms
- `traceroute6` fails for WAN Hosts (both ICMPv6 or TCP)
- Analysis of Wireshark PCAP Shows:
  - DNS Fails (fallback to <requestedhostname>.<MYDOMAIN.TLD>, where search = <MYDOMAIN.TLD>)
  - ICMPv6 seems to work correctly
  - TCP Flags: Lines are either RED (for RST Flag) or BLACK (for SYN Flag & TCP Port Number Reused)
     - RST Flag: present
     - SYN Flag: present
  - TCP Flags missing: Notably absent are ACK or FIN (among others), so the Connection initialized from within the container never gets answered by the outside Services

### Describe the results you expected

Able to reach outside Services normally.

- `curl -6 ifconfig.me` should succeed
- DNS should resolve correctly
- Package Managers (`apk`, `apt`, etc) should be able to download Lists & Updates (when building Containers)

### podman info output

```yaml
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.36.0
  cgroupControllers:

  - memory

  kernel: 6.8.11-300.fc40.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic

      version: aardvark-dns 1.10.0
    package: netavark-1.10.3-3.fc40.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark
    version: netavark 1.10.3
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-1.15-1.fc40.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.15
      commit: e6eacaf4034e84185fd8780ac9262bbf57082278
      rundir: /run/user/1002/crun
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +LIBKRUN +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL
  os: linux

  swapFree: 10656407552

        FUSE library version 3.16.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.38
    overlay.mountopt: nodev
  graphRoot: /zdata/PODMAN/STORAGE
  graphRootAllocated: 298237820928

  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: zfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"

    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageCopyTmpDir: /home/podman/containers/tmp
  imageStore:
    number: 222
  runRoot: /run/user/1002
  transientStore: false
  volumePath: /zdata/PODMAN/VOLUMES
version:
  APIVersion: 5.1.0
  Built: 1716940800

  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 5.1.0
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running on Fedora 40 in a KVM AMD64 Virtual Machine (Proxmox VE).

### Additional information

Routes from within the Container:
```
root@CONTAINER:/opt/app# ip -6 route
64:ff9b:1::1:0:0/96 via 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:66 dev ens18 metric 1024 pref medium
64:ff9b:1::2:0:0/96 via 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:67 dev ens18 metric 1024 pref medium
2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::/64 dev ens18 proto ra metric 1002 mtu 1500 pref medium
2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::/64 via 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1 dev ens18 metric 1024 pref medium
2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::/64 dev ens18 proto kernel metric 256 pref medium
fe80::/64 dev ens18 proto kernel metric 256 pref medium
default via fe80::XXXX:XXXX:XXXX:XXXX dev ens18 proto ra metric 1002 mtu 1500 pref medium

root@CONTAINER:/opt/app# ip -6 route show table local
local ::1 dev lo proto kernel metric 0 pref medium
local 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2 dev ens18 proto kernel metric 0 pref medium
local fe80::XXXX:XXXX:XXXX:XXXX dev ens18 proto kernel metric 0 pref medium
multicast ff00::/8 dev ens18 proto kernel metric 256 pref medium
```

Addresses from within the Container:
```
root@CONTAINER:/opt/app# ip -6 addr
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host proto kernel_lo 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: ens18: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65520 state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    inet6 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:ff15::2/64 scope global nodad 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::XXXX:XXXX:XXXX:XXXX/64 scope link proto kernel_ll 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```

Basically, the only way to get OUTBOUND connectivity is to switch to `slirp4netns`.

This affects both `podman build` and `podman run` (probably also `buildah`).

Further Information available in: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22824

# From Pasta Shell


root@MYHOST:~# curl -6 https://ifconfig.me
curl: (28) Failed to connect to ifconfig.me port 443 after 133000 ms: Couldn't connect to server
root@MYHOST:~# curl -6 http://[2600:1901:0:bbc3::]

```

This:

> ```
> 1283.7822:          Flow 0 (TCP connection): TAP_SYN_RCVD: CLOSED -> SYN_SENT
> 1283.7822:          Flow 0 (TCP connection): hash table insert: sock 654, bucket: 14659
> 1283.7823:          Flow 0 (TCP connection): TCP reset at tcp_conn_from_tap:1730
> 1283.7823:          Flow 0 (TCP connection): event at tcp_rst_do:1379
> 1283.7823:          Flow 0 (TCP connection): CLOSED: SYN_SENT -> CLOSED
> ```

is pasta checking:

```
	if (!bind(s, sa, sl)) {
		tcp_rst(c, conn);	/* Nobody is listening then */
		goto cancel;
	}
```

if it can bind() to address and port used as destination for the connection. If it succeeds, then it concludes that the address is local, and no other process is listening on that address and port, so it can skip calling connect() and simply reset the connection attempt.

Except that in this case, non-local binds (`net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind` and `net.ipv6.ip_nonlocal_bind`) were enabled (that's not the default). If the address is non-local, pasta's bind() succeeds, and we just reset the connection for anything non-local.

Workaround: either disable non-local binds, or disable the if (!bind(s, sa, sl)) ... check in tcp.c. I'll look for a better solution. We can't just check the sysctls at initialisation time because they might change at runtime, and checking them on every connection would cause significant overhead.

Setting `sysctl -w net.ipv6.ip_nonlocal_bind=0` "fixes" (bypasses) the Issue, but I would really like to keep non-local binds, otherwise I would have to setup each IP Address for each Container on the Host Interface.

Using `sysctl -w net.ipv6.ip_nonlocal_bind=1` allows the Podman Container to setup its own IP Address without additional work.

The Proposed quick patch by @sbrivio-rh allow to have non-local bind and OUTBOUND connectivity working:

Replace `passt` git source code of `tcp.c` at line 1729
```
if (!bind(s, sa, sl)) {
```

With
```
if (0 && !bind(s, sa, sl)) {
```

# Minimal Reproducible Example
It would actually be interesting to see if somebody else can reproduce, preferably:
a. Using Fedora 40 AMD64
b. Using the double/triple patched pasta version described here and in the associate BUG Report
c. `sysctl -w net.ipv6.ip_nonlocal_bind=1`
d. Interface managed by `NetworkManager` (hence a. Fedora) probably due to `noprefixroute`

Then try with: 
```
podman run --net=pasta:--ipv6-only --rm -ti alpine sh
apk update
```

The `--ipv6-only` seems key as with `--net=pasta` then `apk update` works (but then it's just using the ipv4 connectivity)

Note however that Alpine's `curl` doen't like to fetch by IPv6 address, so a better way is to just use the Debian's Image, although without the required debugging tools (`nslookup` etc) it's pretty useless anyways because you cannot install `curl` :disappointed::
```
podman run --net=pasta:--ipv6-only --rm -ti debian sh

# DNS Fails so we cannot use this ...
apt-get update
apt-get install curl
```

# Analysis with Pasta Trace
```
# Run as USER
podman@MYHOST:~$ pasta --config-net --trace -l $(date +"%Y%m%d_%Hh%M%S")_PASST_TRACE.log

# It will open a pseudo-root User Namespace Shell where you can run Commands
# Continuous logging to the .log File will occur
root@MYHOST:~#
```

So the issue appears to be UDP-only, now that the patches described have been applied.

From within the Container TCP works flawlessly:
```
# Must use the IP associated with ifconfig.me since DNS Resolution does NOT work
root@CONTAINER:/opt/app# curl -vvv -6 -L https://[2600:1901:0:bbc3::]
*   Trying [2600:1901:0:bbc3::]:443...
* Connected to 2600:1901:0:bbc3:: (2600:1901:0:bbc3::) port 443
* ALPN: curl offers h2,http/1.1
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
*  CAfile: /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
*  CApath: /etc/ssl/certs
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Server hello (2):
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Encrypted Extensions (8):
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Certificate (11):
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, CERT verify (15):
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS change cipher, Change cipher spec (1):
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
* SSL connection using TLSv1.3 / TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 / x25519 / RSASSA-PSS
* ALPN: server accepted h2
* Server certificate:
*  subject: CN=ho.st
*  start date: Jun  5 15:45:21 2024 GMT
*  expire date: Sep  3 15:45:20 2024 GMT
*  subjectAltName does not match ipv6 address 2600:1901:0:bbc3::
* SSL: no alternative certificate subject name matches target ipv6 address '2600:1901:0:bbc3::'
* Closing connection
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS alert, close notify (256):
curl: (60) SSL: no alternative certificate subject name matches target ipv6 address '2600:1901:0:bbc3::'
More details here: https://curl.se/docs/sslcerts.html

curl failed to verify the legitimacy of the server and therefore could not
establish a secure connection to it. To learn more about this situation and
how to fix it, please visit the web page mentioned above.
```

Yes, it's giving a TLS Certificate Error, but `curl` is able to follow redirects and get to the required Web Server.

But of course UDP and DNS Resolution fails:
```
root@CONTAINER:/opt/app# nslookup google.com
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:4#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:5#53: timed out
;; no servers could be reached
```

I don't really have a better way to reproduce. Alpine doesn't have `curl` where you can specify IPv6 addresses (tried with `https://[ip]`, without as `https://ip`, with quotes as `"https://[ip]"` or `"https://ip"`, escaping `\[`, `\]` as in `"https://\[ip\]"` or `https://\[ip\]`, etc, nothing worked).

# Required Files
Simplified `Dockerfile`:
```
FROM debian:testing

ARG TARGETPLATFORM

# Update Sources
# Install other Dependencies / recommended Packages
RUN --mount=type=cache,mode=0777,target=/var/cache/apt,sharing=locked \
    --mount=type=cache,mode=0777,target=/var/lib/apt,sharing=locked \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install --no-install-recommends -y ca-certificates bash curl wget jq net-tools iproute2 bind9-dnsutils traceroute tcptraceroute iputils-ping iperf3 && \
    echo "Finished installing base Packages"

# Change Shell
RUN chsh -s /bin/bash root
RUN export SHELL="/bin/bash"
RUN ln -sf /bin/bash /bin/sh

# set ENV to execute startup scripts
ENV ENV /etc/profile

# Copy and Execute Entrypoint Script
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /opt/
RUN chmod +x /opt/docker-entrypoint.sh
ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
```

Simplified `docker-entrypoint.sh`
```
#!/bin/bash

# Start the main process and save its PID
# Use exec to replace the shell script process with the main process
exec /bin/bash -c "while true; do sleep 5; done" &
pidh=$!

# Trap the SIGTERM signal and forward it to the main process
trap 'kill -SIGTERM $pidh; wait $pidh' SIGTERM

# Wait for the main process to complete
wait $pidh
```

# Build Image
Generate Timestamp for Image:
```
timestamp=$(date +%Y%m%d)
```

Build:
```
podman build --network=slirp4netns -t "network-debug-minimal:${timestamp}" -f Dockerfile .
```
(I must use `--network=slirp4netns` otherwise even the build will fail since I cannot even download packages ...)

Generate PCAP Filename:
```
pcapfile="./$(date +%Y%m%d_%Hh%Mm%Ss)_CONTAINER.pcap"
```

# Run Image
Run:
```
podman run --net=pasta:--ipv6-only,--pcap,${pcapfile} -d --name network-debug-minimal localhost/network-debug-minimal:${timestamp}
```

Enter Environment:
```
podman exec -it network-debug-minimal /bin/bash
```

# Debug Networking
TCP Should work (do NOT use DNS Hostname - this IP is for `ifconfig.me`):
```
curl -vvv -6 -L https://[2600:1901:0:bbc3::]
```

UDP Should fail:
```
nslookup google.com

;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:4#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:5#53: timed out
;; no servers could be reached
```

Or
```
dig AAAA google.com
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:3#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:4#53: timed out
;; communications error to 2XXX:XXXX:XXXX:1::1:5#53: timed out

; <<>> DiG 9.19.21-1+b1-Debian <<>> AAAA google.com
;; global options: +cmd
;; no servers could be reached
```


Or also `traceroute6` using UDP:
```
traceroute6 -U 2600:1901:0:bbc3::
 1  * * *
 2  * * *
 3  * * *
 4  * * *
 5  * * *
 6  * * *

24  * * *
25  * * *
26  * * *
27  * * *
28  * * *
29  * * *
30  * * *

```

Looking at Wireshark Display of the captured PCAP file brings more questions than answers.

For `--net=pasta:--ipv6-only`, `nslookup google.com` only attempts to fetch `A` Records (IPv4).
Using `dig AAAA google.com` forces `dig` to **attempt** to fetch `AAAA` Records as it should be.

Nevertheless, UDP Communication with Nameserver is never answered and no IP Addesses are returned.
